summary: Gruber digs into the backstory of Rudy Giuliani's four Yankees World Series rings, which are now subject to seizure in his defamation lawsuit. Drawing heavily on Wayne Barrett's 2007 Village Voice investigation, he details how Giuliani received the rings while mayor — likely in violation of New York's gift laws that bar officials from accepting gifts over $50 from entities doing business with the city. Giuliani paid only $16,000 for rings worth at least $200,000, and the timing of payments suggests they were retroactive cover rather than legitimate purchases. Gruber uses the episode as a microcosm of Giuliani's character and closes with a pointed comparison to Trump.
key_points: Giuliani is the only mayor known to have received World Series rings, let alone four of them, from a team doing extensive business with the city
New York law bars officials from accepting gifts over $50 from city vendors, and Giuliani's own administration aggressively enforced this rule against others
Giuliani paid only $16,000 for rings worth at least $200,000, with payments made years after receiving them — suggesting retroactive cover rather than a legitimate transaction
Wayne Barrett's 2007 Village Voice investigation exposed the full scope of the graft during Giuliani's presidential campaign
Gruber draws a direct line from Giuliani's self-serving conduct to Trump's presidency, noting Barrett's prescient warning about what kind of president Giuliani would be
money_quotes: The real story is — shocker — a scandal.
If it sounds innocent, wait for the price tag. These are certainly no Canal Street cubic zirconia knockoffs.
Needless to say, World Series rings were not available for purchase by anyone else, at any price.
I've quoted quite a bit here from Barrett's reporting, but there's so much more, all of it crooked as hell — a sordid tale of both large-scale graft and petty grift.
It is not so much that he identified with the Yankees. It was himself that he was serving.
Turns out, we eventually got exactly that kind of president, and might get him again. It's just that his name isn't Giuliani.
Read it and laugh at now-disgraced Giuliani's expense.
key_insight: Giuliani's World Series rings scandal — receiving $200,000 in gifts while aggressively enforcing gift rules against others — perfectly encapsulates the self-serving hypocrisy that defined his career and foreshadowed the Trump presidency.
